description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a striking, angular blade design. The axe features two large, symmetrical blades with sharp, silver-colored edges and a matte black central section, each blade punctuated by three circular holes for visual interest and possible weight reduction. The handle is long, slightly curved, and appears to be made of wood with a dark brown finish, capped at the bottom with a metallic, hexagonal pommel. The central section where the blade meets the handle is reinforced with a metallic collar, adding both structural integrity and a decorative touch. The overall design is robust and aggressive, suitable for display or use in a fantasy or medieval-themed urban setting. The materials and construction suggest durability and a focus on both function and style.
description: A double-headed battle axe with sharp silver blades, a dark brown curved wooden handle, and metallic accents. The design is robust, angular, and visually striking.
description_view_0: Front view showing both symmetrical blades, the central metallic collar, and the full length of the curved handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the blades and the curvature of the handle, with the hexagonal pommel visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the back of the blades and the handle's structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the axe and the alignment of the handle and pommel.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a dark finish, hexagonal metallic pommel, and ergonomic grip; cross-section appears rounded.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, angular blades with sharp silver edges, three circular holes per blade, and a matte black central section.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with a metallic collar for reinforcement.
